questions.q: Rani Lakshmibai was the queen (Rani) of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: She was the Rani of Jhansi, in present-day Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Rani Lakshmibai is celebrated as a leading figure of which uprising?
questions.explanation: She was a prominent leader of the Revolt of 1857.

questions.q: Which British annexation policy did Rani Lakshmibai resist after her husband's death?
questions.explanation: She resisted the annexation of Jhansi under the Doctrine of Lapse, which rejected her adopted heir.

questions.q: In which year did Rani Lakshmibai die fighting the British?
questions.explanation: She died in battle near Gwalior in June 1858.

questions.q: Near which city did Rani Lakshmibai die in battle in 1858?
questions.explanation: She was killed fighting near Gwalior.

questions.q: Rani Lakshmibai is remembered today primarily as a symbol of what?
questions.explanation: She became an enduring symbol of resistance and courage in India's freedom struggle.

questions.q: By what affectionate childhood name was Rani Lakshmibai known before her marriage?
questions.explanation: She was born Manikarnika and nicknamed 'Manu'.

questions.q: What kind of leader was Rani Lakshmibai during the 1857 revolt?
options: A military leader who led her forces in battle
A poet who wrote about freedom
A merchant who funded the rebels
questions.explanation: She personally led her forces in battle during the revolt.

questions.q: Rani Lakshmibai is famously associated with which animal in popular imagery of her last battle?
questions.explanation: She is depicted riding a horse into battle, often with her adopted son tied to her back.

questions.q: Rani Lakshmibai opposed the British takeover of Jhansi after the death of which family member?
options: Her husband, the Maharaja of Jhansi
questions.explanation: After the death of her husband, the Maharaja, the British refused to recognise her adopted son and moved to annex Jhansi.

questions.q: Who was the husband of Rani Lakshmibai, the ruler of Jhansi?
questions.explanation: She was married to Maharaja Gangadhar Rao Newalkar of Jhansi.

questions.q: What was the name of the adopted son for whom Rani Lakshmibai claimed the throne of Jhansi?
questions.explanation: Her adopted son was named Damodar Rao, whom the British refused to recognise as heir.

questions.q: Which British general led the Central India campaign that besieged Jhansi in 1858?
questions.explanation: Sir Hugh Rose commanded the British forces that besieged and captured Jhansi in 1858.

questions.q: The phrase often attributed to Rani Lakshmibai expressing her defiance was what?
options: "Main apni Jhansi nahi dungi" (I will not give up my Jhansi)
questions.explanation: She is famously remembered for declaring 'Main apni Jhansi nahi dungi' (I will not surrender my Jhansi).

questions.q: After the fall of Jhansi, with which fellow rebel leader did Rani Lakshmibai join forces?
questions.explanation: She joined Tatya Tope and other rebels after escaping Jhansi, later seizing Gwalior.

questions.q: Which strategic fort did Rani Lakshmibai and the rebels capture shortly before her death in 1858?
questions.explanation: The rebels captured the fort of Gwalior from the British-allied Scindia, where the Rani fell soon after.

questions.q: According to a famous account, how did Rani Lakshmibai escape from besieged Jhansi?
options: She leapt on horseback from the fort with her son tied to her back
She disguised herself as a British soldier
She was smuggled out in a cart
questions.explanation: Popular accounts describe her escaping on horseback over the fort walls with her adopted son tied to her back.

questions.q: Even her British adversary Sir Hugh Rose reportedly described Rani Lakshmibai as what?
options: The bravest of the rebel leaders
questions.explanation: Hugh Rose is said to have called her the bravest and best of the rebel leaders.

questions.q: In which month of 1858 did Rani Lakshmibai die in battle?
questions.explanation: She was killed in June 1858 during the fighting near Gwalior.

questions.q: The famous Hindi poem 'Khoob ladi mardani woh to Jhansi wali rani thi' celebrating Rani Lakshmibai was written by whom?
questions.explanation: The stirring poem 'Jhansi ki Rani' was written by Subhadra Kumari Chauhan.

questions.q: Before the siege of Jhansi, Rani Lakshmibai had petitioned the British to recognise what?
options: Her own coronation as queen of India
Her adopted son's right to the throne
A military alliance against the Marathas
questions.explanation: She had appealed, including to the courts in London, for recognition of her adopted son's claim to the Jhansi throne.

questions.q: What is the modern-day Indian state in which Jhansi is located?
questions.explanation: Jhansi lies in present-day Uttar Pradesh, in the Bundelkhand region.

questions.q: In which city was Rani Lakshmibai (Manikarnika) born?
questions.explanation: She was born in Varanasi (Benares) around 1828.

questions.q: What was the name of Rani Lakshmibai's father, who served at the court of the Peshwa?
questions.explanation: Her father was Moropant Tambe, attached to the household of the exiled Peshwa Baji Rao II.

questions.q: Rani Lakshmibai spent part of her childhood alongside Nana Saheb at the court of which exiled ruler?
options: Peshwa Baji Rao II at Bithur
questions.explanation: She grew up at Bithur near Kanpur, in the household of the exiled Peshwa Baji Rao II, alongside Nana Saheb.

questions.q: Which loyal woman warrior and companion of Rani Lakshmibai is also remembered for fighting and dying in the 1857 struggle?
questions.explanation: Jhalkari Bai, a soldier in the Rani's women's army (Durga Dal), is celebrated for her bravery and resemblance to the queen.

questions.q: According to a widely accepted account, how did Rani Lakshmibai meet her death near Gwalior?
options: Mortally wounded in cavalry fighting, dressed as a soldier
questions.explanation: She was mortally wounded while fighting on horseback, reportedly dressed as a soldier, near Kotah-ki-Serai outside Gwalior.

questions.q: Which 1957 work by historian Surendra Nath Sen examined the 1857 revolt in which the Rani fought?
questions.explanation: S. N. Sen's official centenary history 'Eighteen Fifty-Seven' is a standard scholarly account of the revolt.

questions.q: After the rebels seized Gwalior, whose forces had the British-allied ruler abandoned, allowing the takeover?
questions.explanation: The Scindia ruler Jayaji Rao, allied to the British, fled as the rebels under Tatya Tope and the Rani took Gwalior.

questions.q: How did independent India commemorate Rani Lakshmibai during the 1957 centenary of the 1857 revolt?
options: By honouring her as a national heroine and martyr of the First War of Independence
By titling her Empress of Bundelkhand
By naming her a Companion of the British Empire
questions.explanation: During the 1957 centenary of the revolt, independent India honoured her as a national heroine and martyr of what was called the First War of Independence.
